data.title: Is Tesla losing its Donald Trump premium?
data.description:
The relationship between Elon Musk and Donald Trump appears to have entered a new phase of pronounced tension and public disagreement. The rift centers on President Trump's tax and spending legislation, dubbed the "One Big Beautiful Bill," and a series of policy and personnel decisions that have appeared to have frustrated Musk, who is very focused on the negative impact on the U.S. deficit of the bill.

This week, Musk unleashed a series of sharp posts on X, calling Trump's hallmark legislation a "disgusting abomination" and accusing it of being filled with "massive, outrageous, pork-filled" spending. Musk argued the bill would massively increase the already gigantic budget deficit and could bankrupt America due to the level of spending in comparison to tax receipts. He even urged his followers to pressure Congress to "KILL the BILL".

Speaker Mike Johnson plans to speak with Elon Musk on Thursday as they clash over President Donald Trump’s well-hyped bill. That follows reports that Musk did not pick up a call from Johnson on Wednesday.

Digging into the tiff, the bill's proposed cuts to the electric vehicle tax credit may have caught Musk by surprise, according to Wall Street analysts. The latest reports also indicate that Elon Musk has frozen Trump's last $100 million commitment to the Trump re-election campaign after the bromance hit its rough patch.

Looking ahead, the public disagreement could be resolved in a flash, or President Trump could use his considerable influence to make things a little trickier for Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX (SPACE) as they look for an easy path with government regulators. 

Shares of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) opened lower 5.9% on Thursday after shedding 3.6% on Wednesday. The EV stock is down 35% from its post-election high of $488.54.

data.title: TOMI Environmental Solutions appoints David Vanston as CFO
data.description:
• TOMI Environmental Solutions (NASDAQ:TOMZ) announced on Thursday the appointment of David Vanston as chief financial officer (CFO), effective May 30, 2025.
  • Vanston held senior finance roles at both private equity-backed and publicly traded companies, including CFO positions at Jon-Don, Flexan, Arcmed-Diba Industries, and VolitionRx.
  • TOMZ is +2.26% to $1.13
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cibus announces pricing of $27.5 million public offering
data.description:
• Cibus (NASDAQ:CBUS) announced on Thursday the pricing of its previously announced public offering of 15.71 million shares of its Class A Common Stock, at a purchase price of $1.75 per share, including to institutional and strategic investors, as well as the Chairman of Cibus’ board of directors (5.71 million shares).
  • The gross proceeds of the offering are expected to be approximately $27.5 million before deducting placement agent fees and other estimated offering expenses payable by the company.
  • The offering is expected to be closed on or about June 6, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund further development of its weed management productivity traits in Rice and for working capital and general corporate purposes, as it pursues longer-term financing.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Silo Pharma approves purchase of Bitcoin as treasury reserve asset; shares up
data.description:
• Silo Pharma (NASDAQ:SILO) has approved the purchase of up to $1M in Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset. 
  • SILO shares up nearly 7% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Mammoth announces strategic alliance, $750,000 private placement financing
data.description:
• Mammoth Resources (TSXV:MTH:CA) on Thursday said it has entered into a strategic alliance with private Mexican company RM Minerías de RL de CV.
  • Mammoth would also like to announce a non-brokered private placement of up to 30 million units of the company at an offering price of $0.025 per unit, for gross proceeds of up to $750,000.
  • Each unit consists of one common share of the company and one-half common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire one common share until eighteen months following closing of the private placement, at an exercise price of $0.05.
  • The gross proceeds of the private placement will be used to advance the company's Tenoriba precious metal project in Chihuahua State, Mexico, evaluate potential project acquisitions and support working capital needs.
  • The pricing of the Private Placement represents a small premium from the 10-day volume-weighted average share price during the past 10 trading days of $0.022/share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow look for direction as China President Xi Jinping and Trump hold call
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages look for direction on Thursday, as reports suggested that Chinese President Xi Jinping and President Donald Trump held talks on the phone.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.1%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.33%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.86%.

Media reports suggested that Xi Jinping and Trump held a telephonic conversation, mostly related to tariffs and trade. 

The U.S. trade deficit narrowed sharply in April as imports plunged.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. 

Productivity growth was revised lower in the final Q1 estimates, and labor costs extended their climb.

The U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts, according to the Challenger Report.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.

data.title: Cellectar Biosciences enters into common stock agreements to raise $2.5 million
data.description:
• Cellectar Biosciences (NASDAQ:CLRB) announced on Thursday the entry into definitive agreements to raise $2.5 million from the sale of shares of the company’s common stock priced at-market for Nasdaq purposes.
  •  The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about June 6, 2025.
  • The agreements provide for the immediate exercise of certain outstanding warrants to purchase an aggregate of 8.30 million shares of common stock, issued by the company on June 5, 2020, October 25, 2022, and July 21, 2024, at a reduced exercise price of $0.3041 per share. 
  •  The gross proceeds to the company from the exercise of the existing warrants are expected to be approximately $2.5 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including working capital and operating expenses.
  • CLRB is -6.21616% to $0.433.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia in focus as BofA maintains Buy after meeting, Chinese companies hesitate
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was in focus on Thursday as Bank of America maintained its Buy rating after a meeting with a management. Separately, a new report suggested that Chinese companies are hesitating switching to alternatives, despite the new export curb controls from the Trump administration.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said the tone of the meeting with Nvidia CFO Colette Kress and an investor relations executive was “very positive,” as demand and customer interest across both the enterprise and cloud remain strong. 

“Importantly, we believe NVDA addressed three key investor debates that have been holding back the stock over the last year: Blackwell rack ramp/execution; AI Diffusion and sovereign demand; and China AI shipments,” Arya wrote in a note to clients. “We maintain Buy, a top sector pick with a $180 PO as we believe NVDA remains best positioned to benefit from the ongoing AI tide, supported by a multi-year lead in performance (AI scaling), pipeline, incumbency, scale, and developer support.”

Additionally, Nvidia's management said the transition to Blackwell Ultra should be smoother than the transition to Blackwell from Hopper, while the ramp-up costs should be “limited,” removing any concern about gross margins, Arya added. 

China surprise?

Separately, the China risk has now been fully removed from its numbers, as there are no China-related sales in current data center forecasts, Arya said, while adding that the rise of sovereign artificial intelligence (as evidenced by the recent sales to Saudi Arabia) are just beginning. 

“NVDA expects each major sovereign to have its own foundational/frontier models, all of which would have to be continuously updated to feed latest info as with any other hyperscaler frontier models,” Arya explained.

Lastly, Nvidia believes that 1 gigawatt of data center installment could be worth $50B across its tech stack. And with between 5 and 7 gigawatts worth of data center capacity having been installed just this year, that could be worth roughly $250B to $350B for Nvidia, compared to its current pipeline of approximately $175B, Arya added.

Separately on Thursday, The Information reported Huawei — which has become the alternative in China to Nvidia given the aforementioned export controls — has struggled with sales to the Chinese tech giants such as Alibaba (BABA), Baidu (BIDU) and others. 

Huawei did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
